item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
can someone remind me if the Doc was always this way?

No, the page was not always as it currently is. 049 received a significant rewrite in 2019.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve been away for some time, can someone remind me if the Doc was always this way? I remember it just being some jerk fancying himself a doctor? I just listened to the audio logs and damn near burst into tears at the pain in its voice as it had its disciplinary hearing.

item.title: Re: Is SCP 049 perhaps THOTH?
item.content:encoded: There's actually a proposal you'll see floating around historical discussions that plague masks may have originally have had beaks so they'd resemble Thoth, because there was a ton of overlap between hermeticism and proto-scientific medicine.

item.title: Re: Is SCP 049 perhaps THOTH?
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if there are any overt similarities. Can you explain?

item.title: Is SCP 049 perhaps THOTH?
item.content:encoded: Has it occured to anyone else that in many way SCP049 is a perfect description of the Egyptian God THOTH??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, I know, BUT, before y’all make fun of me. I don’t care if this convo is older than shite! I just wanna know what kind of font style that is on 049’s page, and how do I use it (as in make the text code)? Simple question please answer, any of you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is really well crafted, love the audio clips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favorite SCP brilliant job keep writing like this!!!! +1

item.title: SCP-5000!
item.content:encoded: I just realized, the evil the foundation tries to kill in SCP-5000's database is the pestilence! The reason SCP-049 turns creatures into zombies is to remove their emotions, and emotion is the pestilence! The foundation used a 'cure' to cure themselves of it, consequently removing emotions and pain. The zombies don't feel pain, and definitely don't think! Plague boi was right all along!

item.title: Re: What the Fuck?
item.content:encoded: The original is archived here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-049-arc

item.title: What the Fuck?
item.content:encoded: i've been gone for 2 years, and now plague dad is nearly unrecognizable

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
o h m y , y e t a n o t h e r v i c t i m o f t h e d i s e a s e

This SCP is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This absolute boi-boi created 610.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ever since I first read the original article years ago this was my interpretation. The pestilence is mortality. I'm not sure whether or not this is a generally accepted head-cannon or not, but I do remember one article about an immortal. As a cross test they bring said immortal to 049, and he is shocked that they have brought him to cure one of the few people without the pestilence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this scp also I don’t know what the “cure” is but I’m thinking it might be immortality…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After reading this again, a rouge thought came into my mind, and I want to know what you all think.

Could the Pestilence be death itself? Now, save your angry response for later, and let me explain.

When first asked about the Pestilence, SCP-049 describes it as 'The Great Dying'. We know that it isn't the Bubonic Plague, and SCP-049 didn't know what it was.

Next, he states that he has the cure to this 'Great Dying'. What is this cure? To make the (now deceased) subject a zombie. He admits that his cure is not perfect. This is because it makes the subject a zombie, and does not simply bring the corpse back to life.

SCP-049 does not kill Dr. Hamm right off the bat, either. This would make us question why the entity killed our doctor. This, in my opinion, is because he was closer to death. Dr. Hamm was getting older, and therefore would die soon. Remember, SCP-049 repeatedly asked Dr. Hamm if he was feeling well.

So, what do you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally, I get to experience the disruption to my suspension of disbelief that other experts must feel when reading an article that misuses jargon from their field! With all due respect, 049 "appears" to be wearing robes of polyester fabric, with a modern machine-sewn hem, and a spray-painted paper pulp commedia dell'arte mask. Take that, STEM majors!

Decent article, though. Keeps the original series flavor while updating the tone and literary quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The old image was incompatible with the wiki's license - hence, the new image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Wait. The archived version is indeed the original, however it has the new picture on it? Is that an error?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there a way we can upvote only the versions we like

Wikidot does not have a system for voting on specific revisions.

This is still one of my absolute favorite SCPs, I just dislike the new writing.

The original is archived here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-049-arc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember that there were multiple opinions regarding if the page needed editing or not when the rewrite happened. Just because you might have felt that it wasn't in need of fixes doesn't mean the author thought the same or is obligated to feel the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed! If it’s not broke, don’t try to fix it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly prefer the original version over all the rewrites. Is there a way we can upvote only the versions we like without having to upvote the version we don’t, or do we have to vote on the latest rewrite only? This is still one of my absolute favorite SCPs, I just dislike the new writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh boi, it's plague dad

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the new rewrite and the added audio logs, I can tell there is so much love and care put into revamping the skip. But I kind of like the old version more due to nostalgia. Anyway, amazing job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully written. The audio-logs intrigued me the most. The mystery of this makes me wonder exactly what this "plague" 049 is talking about, and honestly? The fact none of us will ever know makes it all the more enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How in the hell did I not upvote this yet?

item.title: Re: An Opinion
item.content:encoded: There's only been the one major rewrite.

item.title: An Opinion
item.content:encoded: I really, really, don't like the latest few rewrites. The rewrites before it were fantastic, amazing even, but it's just bad now. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude. You're replying to a 10-year old comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Question:
If someone was to come into contact with 049 but, 049 did not want to terminate the subject. Would the said subject still become deceased?
This question has been bothering me for a quite a while now.

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded:
Darn. Well, thanks for the info.
Oddly enough, I used to be able to listen to the audio files on Firefox in the past. Honestly, I've no idea what's changed.

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: Wikidot hates Firefox, so that may be it.

item.title: Question
item.content:encoded: Is anyone able to listen to the audio logs? For reasons unknown to me, I'm unable to see the audio players used to listen to the audio logs (I've tried both logged out & logged in to no avail). Not sure if it's something to do with my Wi-Fi provider blocking the players or if it's something to do with my browser (Firefox).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, the photo got changed? The old one was so much better. The lighting, the malevolent look in his eyes, the camera angle and how it's not all up in his face, everything in the old photo had more mystery to it. 049 looked creepier in the old one and more…real I guess. In the new photo the mask doesn't even look like it could actually be attached to his face (or maybe that's just me). Both the new photos, especially the second one, looks like it was photoshopped by someone who just learned how to photoshop. When you look at 049 in the old photo, you get the uneasy feeling like it said in the article. The sense of dread you're supposed to get when you look at him. You can tell there was some kind of eerie story behind the plague boi, and people would wonder what this guy went through and how he had gotten that way. The new photos, just, don't have that feel to them.

item.title: Novote
item.content:encoded:
Sigh, I really miss the old photo. It’s black-white color and his lurking-yet menacing glare gave me a very ominous and unsafe feeling of dread.
The second photo is bad enough (in my opinion) that it should be left out of the article. It’s only a blurry photo of the poor guy in a forrest that looks like it was photoshopped into the background.

But I’ll withhold my downvote just because of how intrigued I am with what his backstory might be, and how he became the thing he is today. Maybe those robes and mask weren’t always a part of his biological structure. Maybe they were somehow integrated into his body and became his skin. Maybe the pestilence he speaks of is simply death and mortality, and that the zombies he makes are attempts at making humans who cannot die of age or disease, immune to this “pestilence”. Obviously these beings aren’t anywhere close to what he want’s the final product to be, but it is a start.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A… more reluctant downvote.

I recognize the writing is good. It certainly is but… with the context of it being a rewrite it becomes a -1.

The original 049 mostly worked for me because of the 'less is more' principle. There was just enough information to get a basic idea of what 049 -is- while still allowing the reader to permit a somewhat free interpretation of what 049s exact details are.

This rewrite in that context feels like pulling back the curtain and exposing a bit too much, which kinda makes reading about it feel a little bit dull and as a result the SCP for me kinda lost that original charm of the details being open to interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Top deranged SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this SCP to death (I also love the new updates you guys are making to the page)! The only thing I'm uncertain about is to what extent does 049's touch go to in order to kill? Can he control if he kills someone or does it always occur when he touches someone? Either way, I love this iconic SCP.
+1

item.title: Re: This is gorgeous
item.content:encoded: *Black Highlighter

item.title: Re: This is gorgeous
item.content:encoded: I'm not the author or a designer of the theme, but the nuSCP theme is free for anyone to use.

item.title: This is gorgeous
item.content:encoded: I absolutely LOVE the new look! In fact, I wish all the pages looked like this! It looks much more professional, and the lack of rounded edges makes it seem more like a government document. If you don't mind, could I use the design template for my own articles?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first image really looks handmade, the mask especially. Maybe due to the shine on it, which make it looks like made in plastic matter.
I don't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a moment I thought it was just a really well made picture of SCP-049, but once you said it was a cosplay suit I was amazed at the authentic quality

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let anyone complain about the new images, I dare ya

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short answer: the mouseover zoom didn't look good on it.
Long answer:

the second image was already so big that the mouseover enlarge didn't really help see it any more clearly, and was a little distracting since it took up so much of the page so the odds your cursor would wind up on it was pretty high. It still enlarges, but on click, not mouseover.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Auuuugh the eyes are unnerving as fuck.

+ show block
– hide block

https://gifimage.net/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/tomska-i-love-it-gif-2.gif

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cosplay looks rad Yukki.

EDIT: Why does the first image one pop out if you hold your cursor over it but the second image doesn't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dank.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new images look both authentic and slick as all hell. Honestly some impressive stuff. Great work, Yukki!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an honour to have my cosplay and picture to be used for the official document, hopefully everyone will enjoy them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After nigh on a decade, I finally got around to replacing the CC-noncompliant image with not one, but two which are.

Huge thanks to YukkiAkemi for taking them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very good april fools image.

item.title: Different Way to look at it
item.content:encoded:
Was orignally skeptical of the rewrite, but I realized that if you really wanted… you can interpret the later interviews as him starting to snap into his original article persona and abilities.

Still the rewrite is very well written! +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its true. I had him wear a bag over his head for the first two rounds of drafting entirely.
